steps.title: Identify the clause structure
steps.content: The sentence contains a complete subject (Nery and her colleagues), a time marker (In January 2023), and a direct object (a study showing changes...). The blank must hold the main verb of this independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the finite verb requirement
steps.content:
An independent clause requires a finite main verb matching the tense context. Published is a finite past-tense verb that completes the clause. Publishing, having published, and to publish cannot serve as main verbs—they need helping verbs or other structures to work in this position.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
